column1: description

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/4.8/include/emmintrin.h in the SSE2 implementation of the Intel C compiler (aka GCC) 4.8.x before 4.8.4 improperly rounds the upper bits of the destination, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors.

libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a "stack-based buffer overflow" via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_sse2.c.

The get_user_pages function in mm/vmscan.c in the Linux kernel before 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted mm_struct structure, related to mm/vmscan.c and mm/page_alloc.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the destination vector is properly zero-extended, which allows context-dependent attackers to read arbitrary memory locations via a crafted image, as demonstrated by a crafted webp file, aka internal bug 26403620.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11 does not properly initialize a pointer, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_sse2.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128 and _mm_storeu_si128 intrinsics in GCC before 4.9.0, when -msse2 is enabled, allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted input, as demonstrated by mm_loadu_si128_sse2 in testprogs/x86_64/sse2-loadu.c.
The _do_page_fault function in mm/mmap.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted application, related to mm/rmap.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the destination operand is properly aligned, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, as demonstrated by mmloadu_test.

The _IO_wfile_jumps function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 does not properly handle a certain class of buffer overflows, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fprintf.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11 does not properly initialize a pointer, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_intrin_sse2.c.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a heap-based buffer overflow, as demonstrated by a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_sse2.c.

The smbclient program in Samba 3.5.x before 3.5.22, 4.0.x before 4.0.16, 4.1.x before 4.1.15, and 4.2.x before 4.2.3 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted packet, related to lib/util/tevent_ntstatus.c.

The get_user_pages() function in mm/mmap.c in the Linux kernel before 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted application, related to the mremap_chunk() function.
The get_user_pages() function in mm/mmap.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted application, related to mm/rmap.c.

The mod_dav_svn module in Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x before 2.2.26, 2.4.x before 2.4.17, and 2.4.x before 2.4.17rc2 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a PROPFIND request with a long depth value.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2/32bit/64bit/NEON/ALTIVEC optimized code in libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_sse2.c in FFmpeg before 1.1 does not zero-extend 16-bit values loaded from unaligned memory, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a heap-based buffer overflow, as demonstrated by a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_sse2.c.

The _mm_load_si128 function in SSE2/32bit/ssse3/mm_load_si128.c in Intel(R) C++ Compiler 12.1.0 and earlier, as used in the libstdc++-v3 library in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6 and CentOS 6, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via vectors that trigger incorrect code generation.

The _mm_load_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/4.8/include/emmintrin.h in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) 2.23 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors, as demonstrated by an application that uses the _mm_load_si128 function to read data from an uninitialized memory location.

libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a "stack-based buffer overflow" via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_sse2.c.
The _mm_load_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo in libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted file.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in mm_i386.h in the Intel(R) C++ Compiler 2017 Update 1 (ICC) library in ICC 172.16.58.3 and earlier, as used in the GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) before 8.0, mishandles unaligned memory accesses, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by vectors.c.

The X11 server in X.Org X Server 1.15.1 and X.Org X Server 1.16.0 before 1.16.1 does not properly restrict access to the X11 server, which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka a "root-only" vulnerability.

The vhost_scsi_scan_host function in drivers/scsi/virtio_scsi.c in the Linux kernel before 4.15.1 allows physically proximate attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and system crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted SCSI request, related to scsi_scan_host.

The net_tx_pkt_init function in net/nettx.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of fragments, related to net_tx_pkt_init and net_tx_pkt_add_frag.

The vrend_create_vertex_elements_state function in vrend_state.c in the Vivante GC3100 driver in the Linux kernel before 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and system crash) via a crafted application, aka CID-300055505d01.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the pointer passed to it is 16-byte aligned, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.

The XvQueryAdaptors function in Xvlib.c in Xv in X.Org X Server 1.10.6 through 1.15.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted XvQueryAdaptorsReq request, as demonstrated by xwd.

The _xml_parse_into_struct function in ext/xml/xml.c in PHP before 5.6.26, 7.0.x before 7.0.16, and 7.1.x before 7.1.3 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted XML document.

The smbd in Samba 3.5.x before 3.5.8, 4.0.x before 4.0.16, 4.1.x before 4.1.14, 4.2.x before 4.2.9, and 4.3.x before 4.3.9 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted packet, related to lib/util/tevent_util.c and tevent_util.c.

The Android Open Source Project before 2015-05-01 did not properly restrict the use of the "adb" command, which allowed attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted "adb shell" command, aka internal bug 26403620.

The _IO_putc function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc or libc6) 2.25 and earlier, when the stream is in the EOF state and the next operation is a write, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a write to a stream that has been closed.

The _IO_wfile_jumps function in libio/genops.c in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fwrite.

The _IO_wfile_jumps function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fseek and fwrite.

The Android Open Source Project before 2015-01-01 did not properly restrict the use of the "adb" command, which allowed attackers to perform arbitrary code execution by calling the adb command from a privileged context, aka internal bug 26403620.

The Android Open Source Project before 2015-05-01 does not properly restrict the use of the /dev/binder device, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

The _mm_load_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/4.8/include/emmintrin.h in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) 2.23 and earlier, when SSE2 is enabled, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors, related to mm_loadu_si128.c.

The _IO_wfile_jumps function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fseek and fwrite.

The Android Open Source Project before 2015-05-01 does not properly restrict the use of the "adb" command, which allows attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted "adb" command, aka internal bug 26403620.

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.
lib/libcrypto.so in OpenSSL before 1.0.2h allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted DSA signature, related to dsa_do_sign.

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

The Android Open Source Project before 2015-05-01 does not properly restrict the use of the /dev/binder device node, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

The _IO_file_write_all function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 does not properly handle a write error, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.

The parse_options function in fs/jffs2/build.c in the Linux kernel before 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted ext4 filesystem, related to jffs2_build_xattr_subsystem.

The ssl3_read_bytes function in s3_pkt.c in OpenSSL before 1.0.2h allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large record size, related to the DTLS1_get_record function.

The ssl3_read_bytes function in s3_pkt.c in OpenSSL before 1.0.2 does not properly handle a TLS record of length 2^16+1, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted TLS record, as demonstrated by the TELNET protocol.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in mm_i386.h in Intel C Runtime for Linux before 20150406 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors, as demonstrated by mm_loadu_si128_si.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the destination operand is aligned on a 128-bit boundary, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, as demonstrated by a large number of crafted JPG files.

libavcodec/x86/h264dsp_init.c in Libav 11.10 has a heap-based buffer over-read in the h264_chroma_mc8_mmx function in x86/h264dsp_init.c, as demonstrated by avconv, a different vulnerability than CVE-2014-0971.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the destination operand is properly aligned, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image.

libavcodec/x86/aacdec_sse2.c in Libav 11.10 has a "Shift count greater than number of bits in type" issue, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the "m" parameter is properly aligned, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.

libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_sse2.c in FFmpeg before 2.8.10 and 3.x before 3.3.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by a file with the.vc1 extension.
libavcodec/x86/h264dsp_init.c in Libav 11.10 has a heap-based buffer over-read in the h264_chroma_mc8_mmx function in x86/h264dsp_init.c, as demonstrated by avconv -f h264 -i crash.

The _IO_getc function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.26 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fgets.

The _mm_load_si128 function in SSE2/mm_load_si128.c in Intel(R) C++ Compiler 12.1.0 and earlier, as used in the GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) before 4.9.0, does not zero-extend 16-bit integers to 32 bits, which allows context-dependent attackers to conduct an integer-to-pointer conversion attack via a crafted application, aka CID-300000000d.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a "stack-based buffer overflow" via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_sse2.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-redhat-linux/4.8.2/include/emmintrin.h in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) 2.20 and earlier, when the SSE2 instruction set is enabled, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors, as demonstrated by an application that makes use of the function.

The _mm_storeu_si128 function in SSE2/3/4, AVX, and AVX2 implementations in x86_64-linux-gnu-gcc 4.9.2, as used in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.26, mishandles unaligned memory accesses, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by vectors in the (1) _mm_loadu_si128 function in sse-loadu.c, (2) _mm_storeu_si128 function in sse-storeu.c, (3) _mm256_loadu_si256 function in avx-loadu.c, and (4) _mm256_storeu_si25
libavcodec/x86/h264dsp_init.c in Libav 11.10 has a heap-based buffer over-read in the h264_chroma_mc8_mmx function in x86/h264dsp_init.c, as demonstrated by avconv -i.

The _mm_loadu_si128 and _mm_storeu_si128 intrinsics in GCC before 4.9.0, when -mprefer-vector-width=128, do not correctly handle unaligned memory accesses, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by vectors_sse2_unaligned.

The _mm_loadu_si128 and _mm_storeu_si128 intrinsics in libm in Apple iOS before 9.3.5, iPadOS before 9.3.5, macOS before 10.11.6, tvOS before 9.2.1, and watchOS before 2.2 do not ensure that memory accesses are not unaligned, which allows attackers to read or write data at an arbitrary memory location, related to mm_loadu_si128.c and mm_storeu_si128.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in mm_i386.h in QEMU 2.10.0 through 2.11.1 does not ensure that the value being loaded is properly zero-extended, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by a crafted VDI image.

The _mm_load_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the destination vector is properly zero-extended, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, as demonstrated by convert.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 does not ensure that the pointer passed to it is 16-byte aligned, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file.
The _mm_loadu_si128 function in SSE2 optimized libjpeg-turbo in libjpeg-turbo 1.3.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted file.
libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_template.c in Libav 11.10 has a heap-based buffer over-read in the h264_v_loop_filter function in x86/h264_intrapred_template.c, as demonstrated by avconv -i.

libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11 does not properly initialize a struct, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx_template.c.

The read_header function in lib/libarchive/archive_read_support_format_cpio.c in libarchive before 2.8.6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a crafted cpio archive, related to cpio_read_header.

The jbig2dec library in FreeHand before 0.10 does not properly handle a heap-based buffer overflow, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by pdftoppm.

The netfilter_queue.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) via a crafted packet, related to the nfq_handle_packet function.

The ext4_ext_find_extent function in fs/ext4/ext4.c in the Linux kernel before 4.11.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted ext4 filesystem, related to ext4_ext_find_extent.

lib/libc/stdlib/strtod.c in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, as used in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) through 2.26, mishandles hexadecimal floating-point numbers, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted input string, as demonstrated by an input that triggers an assertion failure in strtod.c.

The _IO_file_write_atomic function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.26 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fwrite.

The inet_csk_route_req function in net/ipv4/ip_output.c in the Linux kernel before 4.4.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, related to the ip_route_output_flow function.

The __alloc_pages_nodemask() function in mm/page_alloc.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15 does not properly handle a failure of an RCU grace period, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a crafted application, aka CID-30000050030e.

The __alloc_pages_nodemask() function in mm/page_alloc.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15.1 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of pages allocated by a single process.

The __alloc_pages_nodemask() function in mm/page_alloc.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of pages allocated by a single process, related to the pagevec_alloc() function.

The xfrm6_tunnel_rcv function in net/xfrm/xfrm6_policy.c in the Linux kernel through 4.11.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted IPv6 packet, as demonstrated by xfrm6_tunnel_rcv.

The __alloc_pages_nodemask() function in mm/page_alloc.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of pages allocated by a single process, related to __alloc_pages_nodemask_node().
The kernel/drivers/gpu/drm/i915/i915_drv.c in the Linux kernel before 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of small allocations.

The xmlSAX2StartElement function in SAX2.c in libxml2 before 2.9.4 does not properly handle namespace declarations, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted XML document.

The _IO_wfile_jumps function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 does not properly handle a certain class of buffer overflows, which might allow context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fwrite.

lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a "stack-based buffer overflow" via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_sse2.c.

The __alloc_workqueue function in kernel/workqueue.c in the Linux kernel through 4.15 does not properly initialize a workqueue structure, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (use-after-free) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, related to the wq_stack_alloc function.

The _IO_file_write function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.26 allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by an application that calls fwrite.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in mm_i386.h in the Intel(R) C++ Compiler 12.1 build 20110304 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by mm_loadu_si128_si.

The _IO_file_write_atomic function in libio.a in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.24 does not properly handle a certain sequence of operations, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, as demonstrated by a file that is created and then deleted.

The _IO_file_write function in libio/genops.c in GNU C Library (aka glibc) before 2.26 allows write operations to a file descriptor with a NULL stream pointer, which causes an assertion failure and application crash.

The bnep_net_setup function in net/bluetooth/bnep.c in the Linux kernel through 4.10.1 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (device hang) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a BNEP-specific Bluetooth device name, as demonstrated by a bnep0 device.

The _mm_load_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/4.8/include/emmintrin.h in the Intel C compiler 4.8 and earlier, as used in the GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) before 4.9.2, does not ensure that the upper 128 bits of the returned vector are zeroed, which allows context-dependent attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory via a crafted application, as demonstrated by use of the _mm_load_si128 function in the libavcodec/x86/xvididct_mmx.c file.
The kernel/drivers/gpu/drm/i915/i915_drv.c in the Linux kernel before 4.10.1 allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) via a large number of i915_gem_object_put calls.

The __memcpy_toio function in arch/x86/lib/memcpy_64.S in the Linux kernel before 4.11.1 has an incorrect size check, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by a memcpy call.

The kernel/lib/idr.c in the Linux kernel through 4.11.10 mishandles certain operations on an idr, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (double free) or possibly have unspecified other impact via vectors involving a large number of allocations and deallocations.
lib/libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 has a heap-based buffer overflow in h264_idct_add_16_mmx, as demonstrated by a crafted file.

The __memcpy_toio function in arch/x86/lib/memcpy_64.S in the Linux kernel before 4.11.1 has an incorrect size check, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by a memcpy call from a 64-bit process to a 32-bit process.
The kernel in Android 4.x before 4.4.4 allows attackers to bypass intended access restrictions via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

The __memcpy_toio function in arch/x86/lib/memcpy_64.S in the Linux kernel before 4.11.1 does not properly handle the case where the source and destination overlap, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (memory corruption) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by a memcpy call.

The __alloc_pages_nodemask function in mm/page_alloc.c in the Linux kernel before 4.15 does not properly handle the GFP_DMA flag, which allows local users to gain privileges via crafted application input, aka CID-0000000003.

libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c in FFmpeg before 0.11.1 and 0.11.2 has a "redundant" "else" statement, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted file, related to libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_mmx.c and libavcodec/x86/h264_intrapred_sse2.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128 and _mm_storeu_si128 intrinsics in SSE2 processors allow unaligned memory access and have been observed to cause segfaults or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted application, as demonstrated by libavcodec/x86/h264_idct_sse2.c.

The _mm_loadu_si128_si function in mm_i386.h in the Intel(R) C++ Compiler 2014 (ICC) improperly reads memory during optimization, which allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted code, as demonstrated by an application that uses the _mm_loadu_si128_si function.

The vhost_net_set_backend function in drivers/vhost/vhost.c in the Linux kernel before 4.4.3 does not properly validate a user-supplied file descriptor, which allows local users to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka CID-000000000001.

The _mm_load_si128 function in /usr/lib/gcc/x86_64-linux-gnu/4.8/include/emmintrin.h in the SSE2 implementation in the GNU C Library (aka glibc) 2.20 through 2.23, when running on a 32-bit x86 system, allows context-dependent attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via unknown vectors, related to mm_loadu_si128.c.
